noServerDescription: Add a RabbitMQ server connection to manage exchanges and routing.
selectServerPrompt: Please select a RabbitMQ server to view exchanges.
noExchangesDesc: There are currently no exchanges configured on this server.
noExchangesOfTypeDesc: There are no {{type}} type exchanges configured.
deleteDescription: Are you sure you want to delete the exchange "{{exchangeName}}"? This action cannot be undone and may affect message routing if the exchange is currently in use.
forceDelete: Force delete (remove even if exchange has bindings)
createNewExchangeDesc: Declare a RabbitMQ exchange to route messages to queues.
typeDirectDesc: Route by exact match on the routing key.
typeHeadersDesc: Route on header values instead of the routing key.
autoDeleteDesc: Removed automatically once the last queue unbinds.
internalDesc: Cannot be published to directly; only reachable via exchange-to-exchange bindings.
argumentsBuilderDesc: Pass RabbitMQ-specific arguments such as alternate exchanges or deduplication settings.
argumentsEmpty: No arguments. Use defaults, or add one below.
argTooltipAlternateExchange: Unroutable messages go to this fallback exchange.
argTooltipMessageDeduplication: Enable message deduplication on this exchange.
argTooltipCacheSize: Number of message identifiers kept in the deduplication cache.
argTooltipCacheTtl: How long (ms) deduplication entries are remembered.
argTooltipCachePersistence: Keep the deduplication cache in memory or persist it to disk.

toast.alreadyExistsDesc: An exchange named "{{name}}" already exists on this server.
toast.amqPrefixReserved: Names starting with "amq." are reserved by RabbitMQ.
toast.serverErrorDesc: The selected RabbitMQ server could not be reached.
toast.createError: Something went wrong. Please try again.

sendMessage.dialogDescription: Publish a message to a RabbitMQ exchange with optional routing key and properties.
sendMessage.dialogDescriptionQueue: Publish a message directly to this queue via the default exchange.
sendMessage.publishingToQueue: Publishing directly to queue "{{queueName}}" via the default exchange.
sendMessage.invalidHeadersJson: Headers must be a valid JSON object.
sendMessage.previewTargetQueue: Publishing to queue `{{name}}` via the default exchange.
sendMessage.previewTargetExchange: Publishing to exchange `{{exchange}}` with routing key `{{routingKey}}`.
sendMessage.exchangeTooltip: The exchange to publish the message to. Exchanges route messages to queues based on routing rules and exchange type (direct, fanout, topic, headers).
sendMessage.routingKeyTooltip: Determines how messages are routed to queues. For direct exchanges, use exact queue names. For topic exchanges, use patterns with wildcards (* for one word, # for multiple words).
sendMessage.payloadTooltip: The actual content of your message. Can be JSON, XML, plain text, or any format. Make sure the Content Type property matches your payload format.
sendMessage.deliveryModeTooltip: Set the delivery mode for the message. Persistent messages are saved to disk and survive broker restarts. Transient messages are kept in memory and may be lost if the broker crashes.
sendMessage.priorityTooltip: Message priority level from 0 (lowest) to 255 (highest). Higher priority messages are delivered before lower priority ones. Note: Priority queues must be declared with x-max-priority argument.
sendMessage.contentTypeTooltip: MIME type of the message body (e.g., application/json, text/plain, application/xml). Helps consumers understand how to process the message payload.
sendMessage.contentEncodingTooltip: Encoding method used for the message body (e.g., gzip, deflate, base64). Indicates compression or encoding applied to the payload.
sendMessage.expirationTooltip: Time-to-live (TTL) for the message in milliseconds. After this time, the message will be discarded if not consumed. Use 0 or leave empty for no expiration.
sendMessage.messageTypeTooltip: Application-specific message type identifier (e.g., order.created, user.updated). Helps consumers route and process messages based on their type.
sendMessage.correlationIdTooltip: Unique identifier to correlate request and response messages. Essential for request-reply patterns and distributed tracing.
sendMessage.replyToTooltip: Queue name where reply messages should be sent. Used in request-reply messaging patterns to specify the callback queue.
sendMessage.messageIdTooltip: Unique identifier for this specific message. Useful for message deduplication, logging, and tracking message flow through your system.
sendMessage.appIdTooltip: Identifier of the application that published the message. Useful for monitoring, debugging, and routing decisions based on the source application.
sendMessage.headersTooltip: Additional metadata for your message as key-value pairs in JSON format. Headers can be used for routing decisions, filtering, and passing application-specific data.
sendMessage.successQueueDescription: Message sent via exchange "{{exchange}}" with routing key "{{routingKey}}" to queue "{{queueName}}". Message length: {{messageLength}} characters.
sendMessage.successExchangeDescription: Message published to exchange "{{exchange}}" with routing key "{{routingKey}}". Message length: {{messageLength}} characters.
sendMessage.notRoutedDescription: Message was published but not delivered to any queue
sendMessage.errorDefault: An error occurred while sending the message.
sendMessage.invalidConfigQueue: Queue name is required for queue publishing.
sendMessage.invalidConfigExchange: Exchange name is required for exchange publishing.
sendMessage.settingsAppliedDefault: Switched to default exchange with queue name as routing key
sendMessage.settingsAppliedRoutingKey: Set routing key to "{{queueName}}" (first available queue)
sendMessage.directDescription: Messages are routed to queues based on an exact match between routing key and binding key.
sendMessage.directRoutingKeyHelp: Use exact queue names or specific routing keys.
sendMessage.fanoutDescription: Messages are routed to all bound queues, ignoring the routing key.
sendMessage.fanoutRoutingKeyHelp: Routing key is ignored for fanout exchanges.
sendMessage.topicDescription: Messages are routed based on wildcard matches between routing key and binding pattern.
sendMessage.headersDescription: Messages are routed based on header values rather than routing key.
sendMessage.headersRoutingKeyHelp: Routing key is typically ignored. Use message headers instead.
